name: Temporal
what_it_does: Durable workflow execution platform that ensures code runs to completion despite failures, timeouts, or restarts. Workflows are expressed as code (Python/Go/Java/TypeScript) with automatic state persistence. Temporal Cloud offers a fully managed service with REST API.
use_cases: Long-running agent workflows that must survive process restarts and infrastructure failures
Multi-step saga patterns with compensating transactions for distributed consistency
Human-in-the-loop workflows that pause for days/weeks awaiting signals
Orchestrating complex AI pipelines with retry, timeout, and branching logic
Replacing unreliable cron jobs with durable scheduled workflows
not_for: Simple fire-and-forget background jobs (BullMQ or Celery are simpler)
Teams without engineers comfortable with Temporal's workflow/activity programming model
Low-latency real-time workloads (Temporal adds orchestration overhead)
Non-supported languages outside of official SDK languages
best_when: An agent orchestrates long-running, multi-step processes that must be resilient to failures, require compensating actions, or pause for external signals.
avoid_when: Your workflows are simple, short-lived background jobs where Temporal's durability guarantees are overkill and the operational complexity is not justified.

auth.notes: Temporal Cloud uses API keys or mTLS client certificates. Self-hosted Temporal supports custom authorization plugins. Temporal Cloud REST API uses Bearer token auth. Namespace-level access scoping available on Cloud.

pricing.free_tier_limits: OSS Temporal is free to self-host. Temporal Cloud has a free trial; no permanent free tier.
pricing.paid_tiers: Temporal Cloud from $25/mo (consumption-based on workflow actions); Enterprise custom pricing.
pricing.notes: Self-hosted OSS is fully free but requires running Temporal server, database (PostgreSQL/Cassandra), and Elasticsearch. Temporal Cloud eliminates all infrastructure management with pay-per-action pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Temporal Cloud rate limits by namespace; self-hosted limits depend on infrastructure

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Temporal surfaces workflow and activity failures with detailed error messages including error type, cause chain, and stack trace. ApplicationFailure allows typed errors with non-retryable flags for fine-grained retry control. gRPC status codes used for transport-level errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Workflow IDs are unique per namespace — starting a workflow with an existing ID is configurable (reject, allow if not running, terminate and restart). Activity retries are idempotent by design via the event sourcing execution model. This is Temporal's primary strength.
known_agent_gotchas: Workflow code must be deterministic — non-deterministic code (random, time.now(), direct API calls) must be wrapped in activities
Workflow history size limit (~50K events) — very long-running workflows need Continue-As-New pattern to reset history
Temporal's programming model has a learning curve — agents generating workflow code must understand determinism constraints
gRPC is the primary protocol — REST API is newer and has more limited coverage than gRPC
Self-hosted deployment requires significant infrastructure (server, DB, Elasticsearch for visibility)
SDK versions across languages are not always feature-equivalent — check SDK changelog before relying on a feature
Signals and queries are different mechanisms — agents must choose the right primitive for external interaction

score_rationale: Temporal is the gold standard for durable workflow orchestration and excels for agent systems that require reliable long-running execution. Full idempotency via workflow IDs and event-sourced execution is a major strength for agents. Broad SDK language support including Python and TypeScript, plus a REST API on Temporal Cloud, makes it accessible. Scores are high but not top-tier due to the steep learning curve around determinism constraints, the complex self-hosted infrastructure, and gRPC being the primary interface. Temporal Cloud's managed option significantly improves operational agent readiness.
